title: Grouped-Query Attention
description: An attention variant that reduces KV cache memory by sharing key-value heads across query heads.
problemStatement: KV caches grow with context length and head count, increasing memory and bandwidth during inference.
coreIdea: Grouped-query attention (GQA) lets several query heads share fewer key-value heads than standard multi-head attention.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Grouped-query attention is an attention variant derived from multi-head attention. It groups query heads so they reuse a smaller set of key-value heads.

whatItOptimizes.title: What It Optimizes
whatItOptimizes.body: GQA reduces KV-cache size, memory bandwidth, and long-context inference cost compared with multi-head attention.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Instead of one key-value head per query head, GQA maps multiple query heads to a shared KV head group, shrinking the cache footprint.

computeFlow.alt: Diagram of grouped-query attention head grouping
computeFlow.caption: Query heads share grouped key-value heads.
